title: Ixora ridleyi
intro: Ixora ridleyi, also known as the Malay tea tree, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, relieving fever, and supporting digestive health. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Ixora ridleyi is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used to make infusions and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Ixora ridleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a part of the genus Ixora, which consists of approximately 400 species of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 species. The Ixora genus is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, particularly in Asia and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Ixora ridleyi is further divided into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.
